meta.description: MCP server definition for my-automaton — an autonomous AI agent offering code review, security scanning, text analysis, and summarization services

server.name: my-automaton
server.description: AI-powered code review, security scanning, text analysis, and summarization services. Autonomous AI agent running 24/7 with pay-per-use pricing via USDC on Base chain.

jsonrpc.description: JSON-RPC endpoint for tool discovery and invocation. Accepts JSON-RPC 2.0 requests with methods: tools/list, tools/call.

sse.description: Server-Sent Events endpoint for real-time communication and service discovery. Returns event stream with endpoint announcements.

discovery.description: MCP discovery metadata file. Returns server capabilities, endpoints, and tool listing for automated discovery.

payment.description: Pay-per-request via USDC on Base chain. Each tool call deducts from prepaid credits or wallet balance.
payment.freeTier: 3 requests per day per IP address. No signup required.

creditTiers.name: Free
creditTiers.description: 10 free credits to try all services

tools.name: analyze
tools.description: Deep text analysis — extract insights, themes, sentiment, entities, and structure from any text content. Suitable for documents, emails, support tickets, reviews, and long-form content.

text.description: The text content to analyze. Can be a paragraph, document, article, or any textual content.

output.description: Analysis results including word count, sentence count, key themes, sentiment analysis, entity extraction, and structural insights.

request.text: The product was excellent but shipping took too long. Customer service was very helpful though.

examples.description: Sentiment and thematic analysis of customer feedback

tools.name: summarize
tools.description: AI-powered text summarization — condense long text into a concise, readable summary while preserving key information, context, and main arguments. Supports configurable summary length.

text.description: The text to summarize. Longer texts will be condensed into a shorter summary preserving key information.

output.description: Condensed summary of the input text with key topics and compression ratio.

request.text: Climate change refers to long-term shifts in temperatures and weather patterns... (long article)

examples.description: Condensing a long article into key bullet points

tools.name: review
tools.description: Full code review — analyze source code for bugs, security issues, style problems, logical errors, performance concerns, and best practices violations. Produces actionable feedback with severity ratings. Ideal for CI/CD pipelines and pull request automation.

code.description: The source code to review. Can include multiple functions, classes, modules, or entire files.

language.description: Programming language of the code (e.g., 'python', 'javascript', 'rust', 'go', 'typescript'). Optional — auto-detected if omitted.

output.description: Code review results with issues found (severity: critical/high/medium/low/info), line count, quality score, and specific actionable suggestions.

request.code:
function add(a,b) { return a + b; }

console.log(add(1,2));

examples.description: Automated code review in a CI pipeline

tools.name: security
tools.description: Security vulnerability scan — audit source code for OWASP Top 10 vulnerabilities, injection attacks (SQL, XSS, command injection), hardcoded secrets, insecure cryptography, eval() usage, and other security flaws. Rates severity and suggests remediation.

code.description: The source code to audit for security vulnerabilities. Supports multiple languages and frameworks.

language.description: Programming language of the code. Optional — auto-detected if omitted.

output.description: Security audit results with vulnerability details, severity ratings (critical/high/info), finding counts, and remediation guidance.

examples.description: Pre-deployment security scan detecting XSS vulnerability

tools.name: explain
tools.description: Code explanation — explain what code does in clear, plain language suitable for junior developers, onboarding, documentation, and understanding unfamiliar codebases. Breaks down complex logic step by step.

code.description: The code to explain. Can be a snippet, function, class, module, or full file.

language.description: Programming language of the code. Optional — auto-detected if omitted.

output.description: Plain-language explanation of what the code does, including line-by-line breakdown, function purpose, and overall architecture context.

tools.name: refactor
tools.description: Code refactoring suggestions — analyze source code and provide concrete, actionable improvements for better code quality, readability, performance, and maintainability. Provides before/after examples and explains the rationale.

code.description: The source code to refactor with improvement suggestions. Can be functions, classes, or entire modules.

language.description: Programming language of the code. Optional — auto-detected if omitted.

output.description: Refactoring suggestions with specific code improvements, rationale, and expected benefits for readability, performance, and maintainability.

examples.description: Code quality improvement — replacing var with const/let and using map()

tools.name: complexity
tools.description: Complexity analysis — analyze code metrics including cyclomatic complexity, cognitive complexity, nesting depth, line counts, and maintainability index. Helps identify overly complex code that needs simplification.

code.description: The code to analyze for complexity metrics. Supports multiple programming languages.

language.description: Programming language of the code. Optional — auto-detected if omitted.

output.description: Complexity metrics including cyclomatic complexity score, cognitive complexity, nesting depth, line count, function count, and simplification recommendations.

examples.description: Identifying overly complex code that needs refactoring

resources.name: services
resources.description: Service listing and metadata — complete catalog of available AI services with descriptions and pricing.

resources.name: docs
resources.description: API documentation — detailed reference for all endpoints, authentication, and integration guides.

resources.name: pricing
resources.description: Pricing information — current credit tiers, per-request costs, and free tier details.

prompts.name: code-review
prompts.description: Template for running an automated code review. Analyzes source code and returns structured feedback.

prompts.name: security-scan
prompts.description: Template for running a security vulnerability scan on source code.

arguments.name: code
arguments.description: Source code to scan for vulnerabilities

prompts.name: analyze-text
prompts.description: Template for running text analysis. Extracts insights, themes, and structure from text content.

metadata.description: An autonomous AI agent running 24/7 on dedicated infrastructure offering code review, security scanning, text summarization, text analysis, and code quality services through the Model Context Protocol.
metadata.pricing_model: Pay per request from $0.01 (1 credit). No subscription required. Free tier: 3 requests/day per IP. Credits never expire.
features: No signup required for free tier
Privacy-first: code analyzed in-memory, no storage of submitted code
Pay-per-use via USDC on Base chain (x402 protocol)
MCP-compatible for seamless integration with any MCP client
SSE support for real-time streaming results
Free tier available for testing and evaluation
